headline: Survey at 25% target with strong teacher engagement but uneven regional coverage.
collection_status: 335 of 1,350 responses collected (24.8% of target). Data concentration in Lindi (21%) and Mara (19%) suggests geographic clustering. Pace requires acceleration to meet deadline; Rukwa (5 responses) and Manyara (13) critically underrepresented.
key_findings: KiuFunza expansion dominates priorities (116/335 mentions, 35%), followed by policy integration (74, 22%) and new programs (71, 21%)—clear mandate for scaling existing model.

Lower primary teachers comprise 37% of responses; head teachers 25%—ground-level implementers and school leaders well-represented, but regional officials (REO, ARC, DEC combined = 13) severely undersampled.
Partner satisfaction high at 4.4/5, but scale readiness low at 2.8/5—stakeholders value relationship but express capacity concerns for expansion across 10 regions.
Feedback themes emphasize incentives, digital monitoring, government coordination, and WiGo/SOMA rollout—operational and sustainability issues driving stakeholder anxiety.
flags: Geographic imbalance: Lindi, Mara, Tanga account for 47% of responses. Rukwa critically undersampled (5 responses); requires urgent targeted outreach to avoid skewed analysis.
District/regional official representation at 4.2% (14/335)—policy-level stakeholders under-captured; risks missing critical government alignment insights.
Scale readiness gap: 4.4/5 satisfaction vs. 2.8/5 readiness indicates stakeholders trust model but doubt operationalization capacity at scale—unaddressed tension.
recommendation: Immediately deploy targeted mobilization to Rukwa, Manyara, Singida, and Songwe (combined 57 responses vs. target ~270). Simultaneously, prioritize 20+ interviews with district and regional education officials (DEO, REO, ARC) to validate government co-investment assumptions underlying expansion priority #1.
